message:
fix(tools): rewrite truncate_head line counting and fix JPEG/session-store edge cases

truncate_head() rewrite (tools.rs + tests):
- Rewrite the line iteration logic from split('\n')-based scanning to
  memchr-based offset tracking, which correctly handles trailing newlines
  and byte-boundary truncation without the previous off-by-one errors
- Fix line counting: empty string now correctly reports 0 lines (was 1),
  "\n" reports 1 line (was 2), "line1\nline2\n" reports 2 lines (was 3)
- Fix byte-boundary truncation to include the trailing newline when it
  fits within the byte budget (e.g., "ab\n" at limit 3 now keeps the
  newline instead of truncating to "ab")
- Add explicit max_bytes==0 fast path returning empty content with
  TruncatedBy::Bytes, matching the existing max_lines==0 fast path
- When first line exceeds byte limit, now return empty content with
  first_line_exceeds_limit=true instead of partially truncating the line
  mid-character (the old behavior could break multi-byte UTF-8)
- Reuse the owned String via truncate() instead of allocating a new
  String::new() in the zero-line/zero-byte fast paths
- Add 60-second timeout to FindTool's child process wait loop to prevent
  indefinite hangs on pathological find commands
- Use inline format string in hashline_edit file-too-large error message

session_store_v2.rs:
- Fix read_line_with_limit() false positive on files where the last line
  is exactly at the byte limit but has no trailing newline — previously
  this was reported as "line length exceeds limit" even though no more
  data followed; now checks fill_buf().is_empty() on the underlying
  reader to distinguish true overflow from exact-fit EOF

terminal_images.rs:
- Fix JPEG SOF marker minimum segment length check from 7 to 8 bytes;
  the SOF segment header is 2 (length) + 1 (precision) + 2 (height) +
  2 (width) + 1 (components) = 8 bytes minimum, and the previous check
  of < 7 could read past the segment boundary on minimal valid SOFs

Test updates (branch_edge_failure_coverage.rs):
- Update all truncate_head test assertions to match the corrected line
  counting and newline-inclusive byte truncation behavior
- Add comments explaining the new semantics for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
